description: Founders, pitch decks, MVPs that aren't actually minimum.

roasts.template: You're not a founder. You're a person with a Notion doc and a dream. The dream is also a doc.
Your TAM is 'everyone.' Your actual customers are 'mom,' 'dad,' and 'mom's friend Janice.'
Your MVP is just V. And the V is for 'Vague.'
You don't have a startup. You have a delusion with a domain name and a Squarespace subscription.
Your 'next Facebook' has fewer daily users than my {item}. And my {item} doesn't even exist.
Your pitch deck has more fiction than a Tolkien novel. At least Tolkien committed to a sequel.
You're pre-revenue, pre-product, and pre-qualified to be in this conversation.
Your runway isn't a runway — it's a runway to bankruptcy with a first-class seat.
Investors don't ghost you. They never saw you. There is a meaningful difference.
You 'bootstrapped' with your parents' credit card. The VCs can smell the parental financing from here.
Your '10x engineer' is you, and you have a 1x work ethic and a 0x sense of urgency.
Your startup doesn't need funding. It needs a refund, a refund, and a refund.
Another day, another {trend} startup. The only thing you're disrupting is the dictionary's definition of 'idea.'
Your 'AI startup' has 47 people on the team and 0 customers. 3 of them are your mom. One is a bot you built to look like a customer. The bot is also leaving.
Your Series A is your mom's credit card. The interest rate is conversations about your future. They're not going well. The card is also maxed out.
Your 'we're like Uber for X' is the most common sentence in startup graveyards. The headstones are numbered. Yours is being engraved.
You have a 5-year exit strategy. The exit strategy is your parents' basement. They've started charging rent. The rent has also gone up.
Your 'moat' is vibes and a Squarespace domain. Both are leaking. The ducks are leaving. The moat is also a koi pond with one fish. The fish is leaving.
Your advisory board is your roommate, your dog, and a guy you met at a networking event who ghosted you. The dog has the most equity. The dog is also leaving.
Your 'next big thing' has been 'next big' for 4 years. The big thing is overdue. So is your refund. So is your therapist's appointment.
Your startup's only 'metric' is your Medium follower count. It's 12. 4 are bots. One is your mom. The rest are recruiters who are about to unsubscribe.
You raise from angels. They're your friends. They're not angels. They're worried. About you, not the company. The company is a concern. The friends are leaving.
Your CAC is higher than your LTV. Your accountant is in therapy. Their therapist is also in therapy. The therapy bills are higher than revenue.
You named your startup after a {food}. There's already 6 of them. Yours is the worst. The {food} is suing. The lawyer is also a {food}. The {food} is winning.
Your 'founder story' is 'I quit my job.' That's not a story. That's a LinkedIn post. The engagement is single digits. The post is also wrong.
Your 'co-founder' is your cat. The cat is also the CTO. The cat is also the only one who has seen the roadmap. The cat is also taking meetings.
